soap:
REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7523 — feat(cache): support batch get-or-insert single-flight
zouhuajian (first_time) · XL (+2744/-116, 6 files)  ⚠ escalation: large-blast-radius

ONE-LINER
  Adds a backend-level batch get-or-insert API (get_or_insert_many) to the Lance
  cache so a single loader can fill multiple missing keys while preserving
  per-key single-flight. Replaces moka's built-in optionally_get_with dedup with
  a hand-rolled in-flight "flight" registry shared by both single-key and batch
  paths, so mixed callers coalesce on the same key. Groundwork for coalesced
  BTree page reads (#6759). Additive; existing single-key behavior preserved.

SUBJECTIVE (author's framing)
  Body is unusually complete: states the two properties it wants (coalesced
  loading + single-flight), why neither existing pattern gives both, the exact
  validation contract, the was_cached semantics shift, and explicitly scopes out
  BTree query changes. Claims local green on stable: cargo fmt / test -p
  lance-core cache (38) / doctests (3) / clippy / bench. First-time contributor.

OBJECTIVE (what I verified against the diff)
  • backend.rs — new types CacheLoadedEntry / CacheBatchEntry / CacheBatchLoader
    are plain and well-documented. validate_unique_keys + validate_loaded_entries
    enforce the contract (missing/extra/duplicate loader keys -> InvalidInput) and
    are reused by both the default fallback and the moka override. The default
    trait impl is a correct compatibility fallback (loops single-key get_or_insert,
    documented as non-atomic and non-coalescing). Sound.
  • moka.rs — the core. Hand-rolled FlightRegistry (Arc<Mutex<HashMap<key,
    Arc<CacheFlight>>>>) replaces moka optionally_get_with. CacheFlight = state
    Mutex(Loading/Ready/Retry) + tokio Notify. Reviewed the three hard properties:
      - No lost wakeup: wait() registers notify.notified() BEFORE reading state
        under the state lock; owner sets state under the same lock BEFORE
        notify_waiters(). A waiter that registers after the owner completes still
        reads Ready/Retry and returns without awaiting. Correct tokio Notify usage.
      - Liveness on owner death: FlightOwner::Drop calls retry() when not
        completed, flipping state -> Retry and waking waiters, who re-loop. So an
        aborted/panicked owner cannot strand waiters. This is the canonical
        failure mode for hand-rolled single-flight and it is handled.
      - Lock ordering: claim_flight takes ONLY the registry lock and never reads
        flight.state under it (comment documents this); owners complete in
        state->registry order. No lock-order inversion between the two mutexes.
    The unreachable Loading branch in wait()/the waiter loops returns a defensive
    Error::internal rather than spinning — good invariant hygiene.
  • mod.rs (typed layer) — get_or_insert_with_key_batch on both LanceCache and
    WeakLanceCache. Type-erasure plumbing (build_batch_keys, typed_loader) is
    consistent; preserves input order; rejects duplicate typed keys. WeakLanceCache
    degrades to load_batch_without_cache when the backend is gone, still validating
    + reordering. Also opportunistically hardens existing downcast().unwrap() call
    sites into Error::internal on type mismatch (defensive, in-scope).
  • Tests (read full patch from GitHub; packet truncates mod.rs at 500 lines) —
    coverage is strong and maps 1:1 to the risk surface:
      - dedups_overlapping_keys, waits_on_in_flight_batch_owner,
        dedups_high_fanout_overlap
      - shares_flights_with_single_key_get_or_insert (BOTH directions): proves the
        cross-path coalescing claim — a batch owner waits on a single-key flight
        for the shared key (was_cached=true) and only loads the disjoint key
      - waiter_retries_after_owner_error AND after_owner_cancel, for BOTH single
        and batch paths, using owner.abort() / pending() with a 5s timeout guard —
        directly exercises the Drop-retry liveness path
      - loader-contract tests: receives_owned_keys_in_input_order,
        rejects_duplicate_input_keys, rejects_loader_key_validation (rstest cases)
      - clear_does_not_cancel_in_flight_get_or_insert, default-fallback path,
        weak-cache-drop paths
  • Cargo.toml — adds criterion under [dev-dependencies] + a harness=false bench
    target only. tokio::sync::Notify is the sole new runtime primitive (tokio
    already a dep). No new production dependency. Cargo.lock +1 line.
  • diff.omitted: none — all 6 files had patches.

ASSESSMENT
  risk: med (refined from baseline med — NOT raised to high)
    Rationale: this is concurrency-correctness territory (replaces a battle-tested
    moka primitive with hand-rolled single-flight), which on content alone argues
    for high. I traced the three properties that would make hand-rolled
    single-flight unsound — lost wakeup, owner-death liveness, lock ordering — and
    each is correct AND has a direct test (including abort()-based cancel). The
    blast radius is wide (cache is on the vector-index + BTree hot paths) but the
    new batch API has no production caller yet (it is groundwork), and the
    single-key path's externally observable behavior is unchanged. Net: med.
  urgency: low (baseline) — no production caller depends on it yet.
  escalation: FORCED on rule large-blast-radius (XL diff) -> full rounds
    regardless of how clean it reads. This is routing, not a risk score.
  CI: red. Investigated: the linux-build failure is a rustc INTERNAL COMPILER
    ERROR (ICE) on the nightly+instrument-coverage toolchain, in
    lance-index/src/scalar/ngram.rs::stream_spill_reader
    (DynCompatible(IndexReader) trait-selection panic, edition 2024). That crate
    is NOT touched by this PR (PR touches only lance-core/cache). The failure is
    environmental (nightly compiler bug in the coverage build), not attributable
    to this change. Java-11 failure + the two cancelled Java jobs are the same run
    cascade. So CI-red is NOT a code condition on this PR.
  could not read locally: I did not run the test suite myself; coverage assessed
    by reading the tests against the implementation. Author's stable-toolchain
    green run is plausible and consistent with the ICE being nightly-only.

DRAFT COMMENTS  (drafts only — never posted)
  question: CI linux-build is red due to a nightly rustc ICE in lance-index
    (ngram stream_spill_reader), unrelated to this diff. Can you rebase onto the
    latest base once the toolchain pin is fixed and confirm a green run? Treating
    this as infra, not a code defect — flagging so the attending knows the red X
    is not on you. (gates nothing about the code; verifies before merge)
  suggestion: The new batch API has no production caller in this PR (groundwork
    for #6759). Consider a one-line doc/TODO pointer from get_or_insert_many to
    the intended BTree coalesced-read caller so the next reader knows where it
    lands. Non-blocking.
  praise: The Drop-based retry on owner cancel + the matching abort()/error tests
    for both single and batch paths are exactly the right thing for hand-rolled
    single-flight — this is the failure mode most such implementations miss.

RECOMMENDATION: approve (no blocking code conditions)
  The implementation is correct on the three properties that matter for
  hand-rolled single-flight, the contract is validated, and the test suite
  exercises the hard concurrency cases directly. Escalation is forced
  (large-blast-radius) so this still gets full rounds / attending co-sign — but I
  found no merge-blocking defect. Recommend approve, contingent only on a green CI
  re-run once the unrelated nightly ICE clears (verification, not a code change).
  confidence: med-high on correctness (reasoned + test-backed, did not run);
    high that CI-red is environmental.
  what would make this wrong: a concurrency bug under a scheduling interleaving
    not covered by the deterministic tests (e.g. loom-style exhaustive check would
    raise confidence further); or moka's get-after-insert visibility being weaker
    than assumed in the owner.complete() cache-hit fast path. Neither observed.
